name: Nikola Tesla
rationale: Inventor whose disputed legacy and lonely death made him a magnet for myth.

name: Grigori Rasputin
rationale: His murder is genuinely contested, which is exactly how a real ambiguity becomes a plot.

name: Cleopatra
rationale: Two millennia of retelling have buried the record under interpretation.

name: Marie Curie
rationale: A pioneer surrounded by rivals — fertile ground for a manufactured-suppression story.

name: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart
rationale: The Salieri rumour shows how a rivalry gets recast as a poisoning.

name: Isaac Newton
rationale: Real alchemy notebooks make an easy springboard for hidden-knowledge claims.

name: Ada Lovelace
rationale: Credit disputes over her work model the 'stolen legacy' fallacy cleanly.

name: Napoleon
rationale: His cause of death was argued for two centuries — a real dispute, not an invented one.

name: Vincent van Gogh
rationale: The circumstances of his death are genuinely debated by historians.

name: Harry Houdini
rationale: A showman who fought spiritualists — an irresistible 'they silenced him' template.

name: Benjamin Franklin
rationale: A web of real societies and correspondents is easy to inflate into a secret network.

name: Thomas Edison
rationale: His real commercial rivalries are the seed of countless 'he stole it' legends.
